title: herniaria hirsuta subsp. hirsuta
intro: Herniaria hirsuta subsp. hirsuta is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the hairy rampion or hairy snipe's-beak. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating gastrointestinal issues. Herniaria hirsuta subsp. hirsuta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
